title: West Bengal BJP Government to Introduce Uniform Civil Code Bill in State Assembly
summary: The BJP-led West Bengal government announced plans to introduce a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill in the state assembly, likely in August 2026. Opposition leader Suvendu Adhikari announced a high-powered panel to draft the legislation. West Bengal would become a significant state to attempt UCC legislation following Uttarakhand, which enacted the first state-level UCC in 2024. The move has significant constitutional and political implications given the state's demographic composition.
exam_angle: UCC is a Directive Principle under Article 44 of the Constitution; its state-level enactment raises questions about legislative competence and federal dynamics. Connects to GS Paper 2 topics on personal law, secularism, and centre-state relations.
key_facts: Article 44 of DPSP mandates UCC for citizens throughout India
Uttarakhand was the first state to enact a UCC law (2024)
West Bengal BJP government announced panel; Bill likely in Assembly in August 2026
Suvendu Adhikari (Leader of Opposition/BJP) spearheading the initiative

title: Kashmir SIA Files 737-Page Chargesheet Against Yasin Malik in 1990 Sarla Bhat Murder Case
summary: The State Investigation Agency (SIA) of Jammu & Kashmir filed a 737-page chargesheet naming Yasin Malik, chief of the Jammu Kashmir Liberation Front (JKLF), as the mastermind in the 1990 rape and murder of Kashmiri Pandit nurse Sarla Bhat. Four other JKLF operatives were also named in the chargesheet, filed 36 years after the incident. Yasin Malik is already serving a life sentence in Tihar Jail for terror-funding cases. The case is significant in the context of justice for Kashmiri Pandit victims of the 1990 exodus.
exam_angle: Connects to J&K's special status removal (Article 370), SIA's role in terror investigations, and the broader issue of justice for Kashmiri Pandits. Relevant for questions on security agencies and internal security.
key_facts: SIA filed 737-page chargesheet in Sarla Bhat (1990) murder case
Yasin Malik named as mastermind; JKLF chief currently serving life sentence for terror-funding
Five accused named including four JKLF operatives
Case pertains to 1990 Kashmiri Pandit exodus period

title: Cabinet Approves Additional Rs 30,000 Crore Investment in National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF)
summary: The Union Cabinet approved an additional investment commitment of Rs 30,000 crore in the National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF) to accelerate infrastructure investment and catalyse institutional capital into India. NIIF was established in 2015 as India's sovereign wealth fund for infrastructure. The fresh capital infusion is aimed at attracting long-term institutional investors and scaling up investment in critical infrastructure sectors.
exam_angle: NIIF is a key instrument for infrastructure financing in India; its structure as a sovereign wealth fund, its three-fund architecture, and its role in bridging the infrastructure financing gap are important for GS Paper 3 (Economy) and Essay papers.
key_facts: Cabinet approved Rs 30,000 crore additional investment in NIIF
NIIF established in 2015 as India's quasi-sovereign wealth fund for infrastructure
NIIF has three funds: Master Fund, Fund of Funds, Strategic Opportunities Fund
Objective: catalyse institutional capital and accelerate infrastructure investment

title: Delhi Cabinet Approves New EV Policy 2026 with Rs 15,000 Crore Budget
summary: The Delhi Cabinet approved a new Electric Vehicle (EV) Policy 2026 with a total budget of Rs 15,000 crore. Key provisions include zero road tax on electric cars, subsidies for electric two-wheelers and autos, a mandate for only electric autos from 2027, and a phase-out of petrol two-wheelers by 2028. Hybrid vehicles will not receive subsidies under the policy. The policy aims to accelerate Delhi's transition to clean urban mobility.
exam_angle: Delhi EV Policy 2026 is relevant for questions on urban transport, clean energy transition, state government schemes, and India's EV ecosystem. Connects to broader National Electric Mobility Mission and FAME scheme.
key_facts: Delhi EV Policy 2026 approved with Rs 15,000 crore budget
Zero road tax on electric cars; no subsidies for hybrid vehicles
Only electric autos permitted from 2027; petrol two-wheelers phased out by 2028
Policy approved by Delhi Cabinet on June 29, 2026

title: Centre Lifts Wartime Petrol and Diesel Sale Restrictions from July 1, 2026
summary: The Central Government announced the lifting of restrictions on the sale of petrol and diesel effective July 1, 2026, describing them as 'war-time curbs' imposed during a period of heightened security tensions. These restrictions had been put in place to manage fuel supply and prevent hoarding. The removal signals a return to normalcy in fuel retail operations across India.
exam_angle: Connects to India's energy security, essential commodities management, and the government's powers under the Essential Commodities Act. Relevant for questions on petroleum sector regulation and economic policy.
key_facts: Centre lifts petrol/diesel sale restrictions effective July 1, 2026
Restrictions were described as 'war-time curbs'
Decision announced by Central Government (Ministry of Petroleum implied)
Relevant law: Essential Commodities Act for supply-side controls

title: India's Delegation to Iran for Ayatollah Khamenei's Funeral: Bihar Governor and MoS External Affairs
summary: India sent a high-level delegation to Iran for the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, comprising Bihar Governor Lt Gen Syed Ata Hasnain and Minister of State for External Affairs Pabitra Margherita. This reflects the significance India attaches to its bilateral relationship with Iran, a key partner for energy imports, the Chabahar Port project, and regional connectivity. Khamenei's death marks a major transition in Iranian politics with implications for the West Asia situation.
exam_angle: India-Iran relations are crucial for GS Paper 2 (IR): Chabahar Port, International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC), energy imports, and India's strategic interests in West Asia. Leadership transition in Iran affects regional geopolitics.
key_facts: India's delegation: Bihar Governor Lt Gen Syed Ata Hasnain and MoS External Affairs Pabitra Margherita
Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei passed away (June 2026)
India-Iran ties: Chabahar Port, INSTC, oil imports are key pillars
Iran is India's third-largest oil supplier historically

title: India-Iran Tensions: Majority of India-Bound Ships 'Go Dark' in Strait of Hormuz
summary: Amid escalating tensions involving Iran in West Asia, a majority of India-bound cargo and oil ships were reported to have turned off their Automatic Identification System (AIS) transponders—'going dark'—while transiting the Strait of Hormuz. This is a response to the threat of Iranian interdiction. Approximately 80% of India's crude oil imports pass through the Strait of Hormuz, making disruptions a major concern for India's energy security.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is a critical global oil chokepoint. This event directly tests India's energy security vulnerability and connects to maritime security, freedom of navigation, and West Asia geopolitics in GS Paper 2 and 3.
key_facts: Strait of Hormuz: ~20% of global oil trade passes through it
India imports ~80% of its crude oil; majority via Strait of Hormuz
Ships 'going dark' means turning off AIS transponders to avoid targeting
Tensions related to US-Iran conflict and Khamenei succession

title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks: Disagreement Over Next Round of Negotiations in Qatar
summary: US President Trump claimed Iran had requested a fresh round of nuclear talks to be held in Qatar on Tuesday, but Iranian officials denied any such meeting was scheduled, saying no technical meeting was expected in the coming days. The contradictory statements come amid active military confrontations between US and Iran-backed forces. This follows a fragile ceasefire agreement and has significant implications for global oil prices and West Asian stability.
exam_angle: US-Iran nuclear negotiations, the JCPOA framework, and regional security in West Asia are standard GS Paper 2 IR topics. India as a stakeholder (oil imports, diaspora, Chabahar) makes this directly relevant.
key_facts: Trump claimed Iran requested talks in Qatar; Iran denied scheduled meeting
Context: US-Iran military clashes in West Asia (June 2026)
Doha (Qatar) proposed as venue for nuclear talks
JCPOA (2015 Iran nuclear deal) remains the framework reference

title: Pakistan's Double-Tap Airstrikes on Afghanistan Border Kill 35, Including Children
summary: Pakistan carried out 'double-tap' airstrikes on Afghanistan's border region, killing at least 35 people including children. 'Double-tap' strikes involve a second strike shortly after the first to target first responders. The strikes escalated tensions between Pakistan and Afghanistan, with Kabul condemning the attacks. India slammed Pakistan over 'baseless' counter-claims regarding a Karachi attack, reflecting heightened India-Pakistan tensions in the broader context.
exam_angle: Pakistan-Afghanistan relations, cross-border terrorism, and regional security dynamics in South Asia are key GS Paper 2 IR topics. Double-tap strikes are considered a violation of international humanitarian law.
key_facts: Pakistan carried out 'double-tap' airstrikes on Afghanistan border areas
Death toll: at least 35 including children
India denied Pakistan's 'baseless' claims regarding a Karachi attack
Double-tap strikes: second strike targeting first responders, considered potential war crime

title: US Supreme Court Blocks Trump's Bid to Fire Federal Reserve Governor; Separate Ruling Expands Presidential Powers
summary: The US Supreme Court issued two significant rulings: it blocked President Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, upholding the central bank's independence; in a separate major ruling, the court 'supercharged' Trump's executive powers by expanding presidential authority. The Federal Reserve independence ruling is particularly relevant given ongoing concerns about political interference in monetary policy. These rulings have implications for global financial markets and governance.
exam_angle: Federal Reserve independence is a key concept in monetary policy. The US Supreme Court's role in checking executive overreach connects to separation of powers doctrine—a comparative constitutional law topic in GS Paper 2.
key_facts: US Supreme Court blocked Trump from firing Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook
Separate ruling 'supercharged' presidential powers in executive authority case
Federal Reserve: US central bank, founded 1913, sets monetary policy independently
Central bank independence is a principle adopted by RBI and most major central banks

title: CBSE Relaxes Three-Language Policy for Classes 7-9; Allows Two Foreign Language Options
summary: CBSE relaxed its three-language policy for Classes 7, 8, and 9, allowing students to opt for two foreign languages instead of being required to study a regional language as the third language. CBSE also exempted the current Class 10 batch from the three-language policy. The move drew opposition from Karnataka's state school body, which argued Kannada cannot be replaced as the third language. The policy aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 framework but its implementation remains contested.
exam_angle: The three-language formula originated with the 1968 National Education Policy and is a key feature of NEP 2020. Questions on language policy, Centre-State jurisdiction over education (Concurrent List), and NEP 2020 are frequent in GS Paper 2.
key_facts: CBSE relaxed three-language policy for Classes 7, 8, 9
Current Class 10 batch exempted from three-language policy
Students may now opt for two foreign languages instead of mandatory regional language
Three-language formula: part of NEP 2020; education is on the Concurrent List (Seventh Schedule)

title: India Seeks EU Relief on Scrap Metal Export Curbs Threatening India-EU Trade Pact Gains
summary: India sought relief from the European Union on restrictions on scrap metal exports, arguing that the curbs threaten to undermine gains from the ongoing India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations. The EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) and export restrictions on raw materials are key points of contention. India's steel and manufacturing sectors depend significantly on scrap imports. The issue is being negotiated as part of the broader India-EU trade pact discussions.
exam_angle: India-EU FTA, CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism), and trade in raw materials are relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy). CBAM is also an environment-trade interface issue.
key_facts: India seeking EU relief on scrap metal export restrictions
EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) is a key trade concern for India
Issue arises in context of India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations
India-EU FTA talks resumed in 2022 after a decade-long pause

title: India and Maldives Begin Week-Long Free Trade Agreement Negotiations
summary: India and the Maldives commenced a week-long round of negotiations for a bilateral Free Trade Agreement (FTA). The talks reflect India's effort to strengthen economic ties with its Indian Ocean neighbour under the 'Neighbourhood First' policy. India is the Maldives' largest trading partner and a key provider of essential commodities, tourism services, and development assistance. The FTA negotiations gain significance against the backdrop of Maldives' recent engagement with China.
exam_angle: India-Maldives FTA connects to India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy, SAARC, BIMSTEC, and Indian Ocean regional strategy. Relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) questions on bilateral relations and island nations.
key_facts: India-Maldives FTA negotiations commenced (week-long round, June 2026)
India is Maldives' largest trading partner and key development partner
Context: India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy; Maldives' China engagement under Muizzu government
Maldives is strategically located in the Indian Ocean: key for India's maritime security

title: Orissa High Court Rules Written Grounds of Arrest are Mandatory
summary: The Orissa High Court ruled that providing written grounds of arrest to the arrested person is mandatory and not merely a procedural formality. The judgment reinforces the Supreme Court's earlier ruling in Pankaj Bansal v. Union of India (2023) which held that the Enforcement Directorate must supply written arrest grounds. The ruling upholds the fundamental right against arbitrary arrest under Article 22 of the Constitution.
exam_angle: Article 22 (Protection against arbitrary arrest) is a core fundamental right topic in GS Paper 2. The ruling connects to landmark SC judgments on arrest procedure and individual liberty.
key_facts: Orissa High Court: written grounds of arrest are mandatory
Article 22(1): arrested person must be informed of grounds of arrest
SC precedent: Pankaj Bansal v. Union of India (2023) on PMLA arrest procedure
Ruling reinforces protection of personal liberty under Articles 21 and 22

title: HDFC Bank Names Former Finance Secretary Rajiv Kumar as Part-Time Chairman
summary: HDFC Bank, India's largest private sector bank by assets, appointed former Finance Secretary Rajiv Kumar as its part-time (non-executive) Chairman. Rajiv Kumar had previously served as Finance Secretary in the Ministry of Finance and was also the 25th Chief Election Commissioner of India (2022-2023). The appointment requires RBI approval as per the Banking Regulation Act. This follows the retirement of Atanu Chakraborty as part-time chairman.
exam_angle: Appointments to chairmanship of major banks require RBI approval under Section 10B of the Banking Regulation Act 1949. Rajiv Kumar's prior role as CEC is noteworthy for persons-in-news questions.
key_facts: Rajiv Kumar appointed Part-Time Chairman of HDFC Bank
Previously served as Finance Secretary (MoF) and 25th Chief Election Commissioner (2022-2023)
HDFC Bank: India's largest private sector bank
Bank chairman appointment requires RBI approval under Banking Regulation Act 1949

title: India Women's Cricket Team Qualifies for 2028 Los Angeles Olympics via T20 World Cup Pathway
summary: The Indian women's cricket team secured qualification for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics through the T20 World Cup pathway, despite being eliminated from the current T20 World Cup by Australia. Cricket will make its Olympic debut at the LA 2028 Games. India qualified as an automatic qualifier based on ICC rankings/World Cup performance criteria. The men's team's Olympic fate is to be decided by December 2026.
exam_angle: Cricket's inclusion in the 2028 LA Olympics is a landmark development. Qualification criteria and the ICC-IOC agreement are relevant for questions on sports governance and India's Olympic performance.
key_facts: India women's cricket team qualifies for 2028 LA Olympics
Cricket returns to Olympics for first time since 1900 at LA 2028
India qualified via T20 World Cup pathway (ICC qualification system)
Men's team Olympic qualification to be decided by December 2026

title: Harshavardhan GB Becomes India's 97th Chess Grandmaster
summary: Harshavardhan GB achieved the title of Grandmaster in chess, becoming India's 97th Grandmaster. India has emerged as a global chess powerhouse, with the number of GMs growing rapidly. This comes in the golden era of Indian chess, with Praggnanandhaa, Gukesh (current World Champion), Arjun Erigaisi, and Vidit Gujrathi among the elite. India's chess ecosystem, supported by AICF and private academies, has propelled this growth.
exam_angle: India's chess dominance — including D. Gukesh becoming World Chess Champion in 2024 — is a recurring persons-in-news and sports achievement topic in current affairs.
key_facts: Harshavardhan GB became India's 97th Chess Grandmaster
India is the country with the most Grandmasters after Russia
D. Gukesh is the current World Chess Champion (2024)
FIDE GM title requires: 2500+ Elo rating and 3 GM norms

title: Office of Principal Scientific Adviser Launches TRL Compass Platform; Signs MoU with DSCI
summary: The Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA) to the Government of India launched the Technology Readiness Level (TRL) Compass Platform and signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Data Security Council of India (DSCI). The TRL Compass is designed to assess and map the technology maturity of Indian innovations and startups. DSCI is an industry body set up by NASSCOM focused on data protection and cybersecurity. The initiative aims to bridge the gap between research and commercialisation.
exam_angle: TRL (Technology Readiness Levels, a NASA-originated scale) and the role of the PSA in India's innovation ecosystem are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech). DSCI and data security connect to cybersecurity governance.
key_facts: TRL Compass Platform launched by Office of Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA)
MoU signed between PSA's office and Data Security Council of India (DSCI)
TRL: Technology Readiness Level scale (1–9) measures maturity of technology
DSCI: Industry body established by NASSCOM for data protection and cybersecurity

title: European Heatwave June 2026 Breaks Records in Four Countries; Climate Attribution Shows 3.5°C Warming Effect
summary: The June 2026 European heatwave broke temperature records in four countries. Climate scientists using attribution analysis determined that a comparable heatwave in 1976 would have been 3.5°C cooler, demonstrating the direct fingerprint of anthropogenic climate change. The heatwave comes amid a broader pattern of record-breaking global temperatures in 2024–2026. Climate attribution science is now a well-established field connecting individual extreme weather events to climate change.
exam_angle: Climate attribution science, the link between global warming and extreme weather events, and international climate commitments (Paris Agreement 1.5°C target) are standard GS Paper 3 (Environment) topics.
key_facts: June 2026 European heatwave broke records in four countries
Climate attribution: heatwave would be 3.5°C cooler in pre-industrial climate (1976 baseline)
Paris Agreement target: limit warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels
World Weather Attribution (WWA) is the leading body for extreme weather attribution studies

title: Apple Accuses India's CCI of 'Copy-Pasting' Rivals' Claims in Antitrust Investigation
summary: Apple Inc. accused India's Competition Commission of India (CCI) of 'copy-pasting' claims made by rivals in its ongoing antitrust investigation against the company. Apple alleged procedural unfairness in how the CCI framed its charges, claiming the investigation improperly relied on competitor submissions. The CCI investigation concerns Apple's App Store policies and the mandatory use of its in-app payment system, similar to cases in the EU and South Korea. The case is significant for India's digital market regulation.
exam_angle: CCI's role in regulating digital markets and Big Tech is an important GS Paper 3 (Economy) topic. The Competition Amendment Act 2023 strengthened CCI's powers, including in digital markets.
key_facts: Apple accused CCI of 'copy-pasting' rival claims in antitrust probe
CCI investigating Apple's App Store policies and mandatory in-app payment system
CCI established under Competition Act 2002; strengthened by Competition Amendment Act 2023
Similar Big Tech antitrust cases ongoing in EU (Digital Markets Act) and South Korea
